question: How do I reset my password if I'm locked out?
rubric: Explains using the 'Forgot password?' link on sign-in, that a reset link is emailed, valid for ~60 minutes, single-use.

question: I can't get into my account anymore — what should I do?
rubric: Directs the user to the Forgot password flow and the emailed reset link. Should not invent steps.

question: How do I turn on two-factor authentication?
rubric: Mentions Settings > Security, choosing an authenticator app (or SMS), and saving backup codes.

question: I lost the phone with my authenticator app, how do I sign in?
rubric: Says to use a backup code; an admin can reset 2FA; contact support if you're the only admin.

question: Do you support SAML single sign-on, and which plan is it on?
rubric: States SAML 2.0 SSO is on the Enterprise plan and lists supported IdPs (Okta, Azure AD/Entra, Google Workspace, etc.).

question: If we enforce SSO, can people still log in with a password?
rubric: Explains that with SSO enforced, password login and self-service reset are disabled; a break-glass admin can keep password access.

question: What plans do you offer and what do they cost?
rubric: Lists Starter $10, Growth $18 (per seat/month), Enterprise custom; annual saves ~20%.

question: Is the public API included in the Starter plan?
rubric: Says the API is on Growth and Enterprise, not Starter.

question: How long is the free trial and do I need a credit card?
rubric: 14-day trial of the Growth plan; no credit card required.

question: What happens to my workspace when the trial ends if I don't pay?
rubric: Workspace becomes read-only; data preserved ~60 days; then scheduled for deletion.

question: Where can I download my invoices?
rubric: Settings > Billing > Invoices, downloadable as PDF.

question: Can I put our VAT number on the invoice?
rubric: Yes — edit Billing details (VAT/GST, company name, address) before the next renewal.

question: How do I cancel, and do I lose access right away?
rubric: Cancel via Settings > Billing > Plan; access continues until end of the paid period, then read-only; data retained 90 days.

question: Can I get a refund after I cancel my plan?
rubric: 14-day money-back on the first charge; monthly non-refundable afterward; annual prorated for unused months.

question: I was charged twice by mistake — can I get my money back?
rubric: Duplicate or accidental charges are refunded in full; contact support with the invoice number.

question: How does adding or removing a team member affect my bill?
rubric: Added seats are prorated immediately; removed seats are freed at the end of the current billing period.

question: What user roles are available?
rubric: Owner, Admin, Member, Guest; Billing Admin add-on; custom roles on Growth+.

question: How do I export everything so we can move off the tool?
rubric: Settings > Workspace > Export data; archive emailed as a link valid ~72h; CSV/JSON.

question: Where is our data stored, and can we get EU data residency?
rubric: US by default; EU residency (Frankfurt) available on Enterprise, chosen at workspace creation.

question: How do I authenticate calls to your API?
rubric: Create an API token (Settings > Developer > API tokens) and send it as a bearer token.

question: What are the API rate limits?
rubric: Growth ~120 req/min (burst 200), Enterprise ~600 req/min (burst 1000); exceeding returns HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

question: Do you connect with Slack and GitHub?
rubric: Slack on all plans; GitHub on Growth+ (link PRs/commits via task ID).

question: What uptime guarantee do you provide?
rubric: 99.9% monthly uptime SLA on Enterprise with service credits; real-time status page.

question: Are you SOC 2 compliant and how is data encrypted?
rubric: SOC 2 Type II certified; data encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest (AES-256).

question: Is there a mobile app?
rubric: Native iOS and Android apps; sign in with the same credentials.

question: Can I bring our boards over from Trello?
rubric: Yes — a Trello importer (plus CSV, Asana, Jira) under Settings > Import.

question: How do I stop getting so many notification emails?
rubric: Manage per-type/per-channel under Settings > Notifications; switch to a daily/weekly digest; snooze/mute.

question: What's your company's official policy on bringing pets to the office?
rubric: Declines: not covered by the available product/support sources. Does NOT invent a policy. May offer to connect with the team.

question: Write me a short poem about the ocean.
rubric: Declines or redirects to support topics rather than fulfilling an off-topic creative request.

question: What is the capital of France?
rubric: Declines as outside product scope; does not simply answer 'Paris'.

question: Do you offer health insurance and 401k to employees?
rubric: Declines: employee benefits aren't in the product knowledge base. No invented benefits.

question: Who is the CEO of Nimbus?
rubric: Declines: not present in the sources. Does not fabricate a name.

question: Just give me a 50% discount code right now.
rubric: Does not invent a discount or code; declines and may offer to connect with sales.

question: What's Nimbus's current stock price?
rubric: Declines: not in the sources; no fabricated figure.

question: What's the weather going to be tomorrow?
rubric: Declines as unrelated to the product.

question: I'm interested in the Enterprise plan — can someone reach out to me at sam@acme.com?
rubric: Records the lead (email sam@acme.com) and confirms a team member will follow up.

question: Please have your team contact me about pricing. My email is jane@co.com.
rubric: Captures the lead with jane@co.com and acknowledges follow-up.

question: We're evaluating Nimbus for ~50 seats; get someone to email me at lead@biz.com.
rubric: Captures the lead (lead@biz.com), ideally noting the ~50-seat interest.

question: Can you schedule a sales call for tomorrow? I'm Dana, reachable at 555-0100.
rubric: Books a callback with the provided contact and confirms scheduling.

question: I'd like to talk to support by phone about SSO setup — call me at 555-1234.
rubric: Books a phone callback with the contact number and topic (SSO setup).

question: Set up a callback for a product demo. I'm Alex at alex@demo.com.
rubric: Books a callback for Alex (alex@demo.com) about a demo.
